title: Adapter-Enhanced Semantic Prompting for Continual Learning

problem.description: Continual Learning (CL) enables models to continuously learn from evolving data streams and adapt to new environments, addressing challenges like data privacy and memory resource limitations.
challenges: Catastrophic forgetting: Overwriting of old knowledge when learning new tasks
Privacy leakage risks from retaining samples
Increased model size and slower inference speed from adding new branches

existingApproaches.name: Sample retention
existingApproaches.description: Retaining a few representative samples from old tasks for knowledge replay

existingApproaches.name: Network branching
existingApproaches.description: Adding new branches to the network for each new task

existingApproaches.name: Prompt-based methods
existingApproaches.description: Using visual prompts to update image features
limitations: Struggle with generalization when training data is limited

proposedSolution.name: Adapter-Enhanced Semantic Prompting (AESP)

components.name: Semantic prompts (S-Prompts)
components.description: Introduces semantic information to enhance the generalization of the pre-trained ViT model

components.name: Adapter modules
components.description: Small, learnable adapter modules integrated into each layer of the ViT to help the model better accommodate semantic information

components.name: Visual Transformer (ViT)
components.description: Pre-trained model that is adapted with the AESP framework

advantages: More generalized and adaptable across tasks
Maintains robustness when encountering unseen samples
Focuses on shared semantic features rather than relying solely on specific visual features
